content: Just as human language learners use written reflective journaling (the lexical sense of reflexion from high English curricula) to refine grammatical and argumentative skills over repeated practice, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can borrow the structured, scaffolded reflexion routines from second-language writing pedagogy to improve their ability to correct reasoning errors. This creates a new training framework where incremental, guided reflexion on output, modeled on how humans learn to refine their communication, outperforms generic unstructured self-reflection for LLMs.
